shortAnswer: Interleukin-6 is an immune system messenger that rises early in acute inflammation and infection, often before CRP is measurably elevated. It's mainly used in hospitals to assess severe inflammatory reactions early.
metaDescription: Interleukin-6 (IL-6) elevated? Meaning as an early inflammation marker explained.
doctorQuestions: Why was IL-6 measured in addition to CRP?
How severe is my inflammatory reaction?
